work_unit: SZ-SP-M-002: getHistory sendMessage lacks .catch — permanent loading state
situation: HistoryTab.tsx refresh() calls chrome.runtime.sendMessage({action:'getHistory'}).then(...) with no .catch handler. If background script throws or sender disconnects, setLoading(false) never executes → UI stuck on '加载中...' forever. Same pattern in handleDelete and handleClear (async/await without try/catch).
action: Added .catch/.finally to refresh() getHistory call so setLoading(false) always runs. Wrapped handleDelete and handleClear in try/catch blocks to prevent unhandled rejections from propagating.
outcome_evidence: Committed bf9842c on auto/opt-sidepanel-1782907225, pushed to origin. File HistoryTab.tsx now has .catch(err => console.error(...)).finally(() => setLoading(false)) on getHistory, and try/catch on deleteHistory/clearHistory handlers.
next_suggestion: SZ-SP-M-003: refresh() stale closure — no mounted check, double-fetch in StrictMode
SZ-SP-M-004: Progress listener race condition — interleaved events from concurrent analyses

audit_findings.finding: chrome.runtime.sendMessage getHistory promise chain has .then but no .catch — IPC failure leaves UI in permanent loading state
audit_findings.remediation: Add .catch handler + .finally(() => setLoading(false)) to guarantee loading state clears

audit_findings.finding: handleDelete awaits sendMessage without try/catch — rejection propagates unhandled
handleClear awaits sendMessage without try/catch — rejection propagates unhandled
